title: scaevola micrantha
intro: Scaevola micrantha, also known as the small blue flower or sea lavender, is a perennial plant native to Australia, known for its blue or purple flowers and medicinal properties. It has been traditionally used to treat various health issues due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate respiratory problems, wounds, and skin conditions. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and promoting wound healing, making it a valuable remedy in herbal medicine. Scaevola micrantha can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
